title: The Case for Year-Round Schooling
subtitle: 取消暑假？全年制学校正在兴起

paragraphs.en: The traditional school year, with three months of vacation every summer, was first implemented when America was an agricultural society and the summer months were needed for farm work. Since then, we've completely changed as a nation. Students no longer spend summers farming, but they aren't in school, either. The average American student receives 13 weeks off from school each calendar year — with about 11 of those during the summer. Few other countries have more than seven weeks off in a school calendar.
paragraphs.zh: 传统的学年制包含每年夏天三个月的假期，这一制度最初实施时，美国尚处于农业社会，夏季月份需用于干农活。自那时起，我们整个国家已彻底改变。学生不再需要夏天务农，但他们也并未待在学校里。美国学生平均每学年享有13周假期，其中约11周集中在夏季。其他国家的学年假期很少有超过七周的。

paragraphs.en: With the US lagging behind countries in academics, it's time to consider year-round schooling. One benefit of this change is that students will not fall victim to the "summer slide," or the well-documented phenomenon where students forget some of the knowledge they have acquired when too much time is taken off from school. Decades of research shows that it can take from 8 to 13 weeks at the beginning of every school year for students to get back to where they were before the summer holiday.
paragraphs.zh: 鉴于美国在学术方面已落后于其他国家，是时候考虑实行全年制教学了。这一转变的一个好处是，学生将不再遭受"暑期滑坡"之苦，这一现象已有详实记录，即学生因离开学校时间过长而遗忘了部分所学知识。数十年的研究表明，每个新学年开始时，学生可能需要八到十三周的时间才能恢复到暑假前的学业水平。

words.meaning: adj. 有大量文件记载的；证据充分的

paragraphs.en: But year-round schooling isn't just about academics. Teachers and students experience a closer relationship in year-round schools than they do in traditional schools and, in the absence of any long-term break, students do not feel detached from the school environment. These closer bonds and greater attachment pay off. Research shows that students in year-round schools are more self-confident and feel more positive about their schooling experience.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，全年制教学的好处并不仅限于学业。在全年制学校中，师生关系比传统学校更为密切，而且由于没有长时间的中断，学生不会感到与学校环境疏离。这种更紧密的联系和更强的归属感会带来回报。研究表明，全年制学校的学生更加自信，对自己的求学体验也抱有更积极的态度。

paragraphs.en: But don't kids need time to relax? Some childhood development experts believe that time off from school is vital to healthy development as kids are not designed to spend so much of their time inside classrooms and the summer break provides a perfect opportunity to get outside. The problem with this argument is that most children aren't playing outside or even spending time with other kids. While some children visit summer camps, most stay at home, watching TV or playing games on electronic devices, which hardly benefits them. The US has changed from a farming economy to a knowledge- and innovation-based economy, so it makes sense for the school year to change as well.
paragraphs.zh: 可是，孩子们难道不需要时间放松吗？一些儿童发展专家认为，假期对健康成长至关重要，因为孩子天性并不适合在教室里待那么久，而暑假正提供了到户外活动的绝佳机会。这种观点的问题在于，现在大多数孩子并不在外面玩耍，甚至都不和其他孩子待在一起。虽然有些孩子会去参加夏令营，但大多数都待在家里看电视或玩电子设备，这对他们几乎毫无益处。美国已经从农业经济转变为以知识和创新为基础的经济，因此学制随之改变也是理所当然的。
